name: Google Chrome
description: Dominant web browser with V8 engine, massive extension library, and deep Google service integration.

name: Firefox
description: Privacy-focused open-source browser by Mozilla. Container tabs, tracking protection, and strong extension support.

name: Safari
description: Apple's browser. Best battery life on Mac, tight ecosystem integration, and strong privacy defaults.

name: Brave
description: Chromium-based browser with built-in ad/tracker blocking, optional crypto rewards, and Tor integration.

name: Arc
description: Reimagined browser by The Browser Company. Sidebar tabs, spaces, split views, and built-in tools.

name: Vivaldi
description: Highly customizable Chromium browser. Tab stacking, built-in mail/calendar, and a focus on power users.

name: Microsoft Edge
description: Microsoft's Chromium-based browser. Good performance, vertical tabs, and collections for research.

name: Discord
description: Voice, video, and text chat for communities. Servers, channels, threads, bots, and screen sharing.

name: Slack
description: Workplace messaging with channels, threads, integrations, and workflow automations.

name: Microsoft Teams
description: Workplace communication hub with chat, video meetings, file sharing, and Office 365 integration.

name: Telegram
description: Cloud-based messenger with large groups, channels, bots, and fast file sharing. 2GB file uploads.

name: Signal
description: End-to-end encrypted messenger. Open-source, privacy-first, with disappearing messages and sealed sender.

name: Element
description: Matrix client for decentralized, end-to-end encrypted communication. Self-hostable and federated.

name: Zoom
description: Video conferencing platform. Reliable meetings, webinars, screen sharing, and breakout rooms.

name: Mullvad VPN
description: Privacy-first VPN. No email required, anonymous accounts, accepts cash. Audited and transparent.

name: Proton VPN
description: VPN by Proton. Free tier with no data limits, Swiss jurisdiction, and Secure Core servers.

name: Tailscale
description: Zero-config mesh VPN built on WireGuard. Connect your devices and servers in a private network.

name: WireGuard
description: Modern VPN protocol. Extremely simple, fast, and lean. Built into the Linux kernel.

name: NordVPN
description: Popular consumer VPN with a large server network, double VPN, and dedicated IP options.

name: Chess.com
description: Largest online chess platform. Play, learn, solve puzzles, and watch tournaments. Huge community.

name: Lichess
description: Free, open-source chess server. No ads, no paywalls. Analysis board, puzzles, and tournaments.

name: Chessable
description: Chess learning platform using spaced repetition. Master openings, tactics, and endgames with science-backed methods.

name: Stockfish
description: Strongest open-source chess engine. Used for analysis in virtually every chess tool and platform.

name: Arena Chess GUI
description: Free chess GUI for Windows. Supports UCI/WinBoard engines, databases, and tournament organization.

name: qBittorrent
description: Free, open-source BitTorrent client. Clean interface, no ads, built-in search, and sequential downloading.

name: Transmission
description: Lightweight, open-source BitTorrent client. Minimal resource usage with a clean, simple interface.

name: Deluge
description: Free, open-source BitTorrent client with a plugin system. Runs as daemon with web UI or desktop client.

name: rTorrent
description: Terminal-based BitTorrent client for power users. Lightweight, scriptable, often paired with ruTorrent web UI.

name: BiglyBT
description: Feature-rich, open-source BitTorrent client. Swarm merging, I2P support, and detailed torrent analytics.

name: uTorrent
description: Compact BitTorrent client. Once the most popular torrent app, now ad-supported with a web-based version.

name: Waterfox
description: Privacy-focused Firefox fork. Supports legacy Firefox extensions, disables telemetry, and offers more user control.

name: Zen Browser
description: Firefox-based browser focused on privacy and customization. Sidebar tabs, split views, and a clean modern interface.

name: Floorp
description: Customizable Firefox-based browser from Japan. Vertical tabs, workspaces, flexible toolbar, and strong privacy defaults.

name: Opera
description: Feature-rich Chromium browser with built-in VPN, ad blocker, and integrated messengers. 30+ years of development.

name: Orion
description: WebKit-based browser by Kagi with native speed on Apple platforms. Supports Chrome, Firefox, and Safari extensions simultaneously.

name: Mullvad Browser
description: Privacy browser built with the Tor Project. Anti-fingerprinting, no telemetry, and no third-party tracking. Free and open-source.

name: LibreWolf
description: Privacy-hardened Firefox fork. Removes telemetry, disables DRM, and ships with uBlock Origin. Community-maintained.

name: Ladybird
description: Independent browser engine built from scratch, not forked from existing engines. Funded by donations, no ads or data collection.

name: Min
description: Fast, minimal browser focused on privacy. Built-in ad/tracker blocking, tab grouping, and full-text search of browsing history.

name: WhatsApp
description: End-to-end encrypted messaging app by Meta. Text, voice, video calls, groups, and file sharing for billions of users.

name: Threema
description: Privacy-focused messenger from Switzerland. No phone number required, end-to-end encrypted, and independently audited.

name: Wire
description: Secure collaboration platform with end-to-end encryption. Messaging, calls, file sharing, and guest rooms for teams.

name: Mattermost
description: Self-hosted team messaging platform. Channels, playbooks, integrations, and on-premise deployment for security-conscious orgs.

name: Zulip
description: Threaded team chat for async-first communication. Topic-based organization, open-source, and self-hostable.

name: Rocket.Chat
description: Self-hosted team communication with messaging, voice, video, and federation. Air-gapped and on-premise deployment options.

name: Mumble
description: Free, open-source voice chat with low latency and high quality. Self-hostable with positional audio and strong encryption.

name: Guilded
description: Community chat platform with built-in event calendars, forums, and scheduling. Free with gaming-focused features.

name: SimpleX Chat
description: Private messenger with no user IDs or phone numbers. End-to-end encrypted, decentralized, and independently audited.

name: ExpressVPN
description: Premium VPN service with servers in 105 countries. Fast speeds, TrustedServer technology, and split tunneling.

name: Surfshark
description: Budget-friendly VPN with unlimited device connections. CleanWeb ad blocker, MultiHop, and Nexus server network.

name: IVPN
description: Privacy-focused VPN based in Gibraltar. Open-source apps, no email required to sign up, and transparency reports.

name: SCID vs. PC
description: Free chess database and analysis tool. Manage PGN databases, run engine analysis, and study games offline.

name: CuteChess
description: Cross-platform chess GUI for engine tournaments and matches. Supports UCI and XBoard protocols with PGN output.

name: Lucas Chess
description: Free chess training program with many built-in engines. Progressive difficulty, tactics training, and game analysis.

name: python-chess
description: Pure Python chess library with move generation, validation, PGN/SVG rendering, and Stockfish/UCI engine communication.

name: ChessTempo
description: Online chess tactics trainer with adaptive difficulty. Endgame practice, opening trainer, and a large problem database.

name: Leela Chess Zero
description: Neural network chess engine inspired by AlphaZero. Learns through self-play with no human bias. GPLv3 licensed.

name: ChessBase
description: Industry-standard chess database and analysis software. 8M+ games, opening training, video courses, and Fritz engine.

name: En Croissant
description: Open-source, cross-platform chess GUI. Game analysis with eval graphs, personal database, and built-in engine downloads.

name: Nibbler
description: Real-time analysis GUI for Leela Chess Zero. Displays engine evaluations, winrate graphs, and top moves continuously.

name: Chess King
description: Chess education and analysis software. Tactics courses, opening trainers, and engine analysis for PC and Mac.

name: Shredder Chess
description: 19-time World Computer Chess Champion. Adjustable playing strength, analysis tools, and online play across all platforms.

name: Fritz
description: Long-running chess engine and GUI by ChessBase. Human-like play style, beauty points for aggressive moves, and online play.

name: PyChess
description: GTK chess client for Linux and Windows. Play against engines, online via FICS/ICC, with PGN support and variants.

name: ChessX
description: Free, open-source chess database. Manage PGN collections, search by position, analyze with engines, and study openings.

name: Chessify
description: Cloud chess analysis platform. Run Stockfish and Leela at up to 1B nodes/sec, with a 9.7M game database.

name: chess-tui
description: Terminal chess game in Rust. Play locally, against UCI engines, or online via Lichess. Customizable themes.

name: Lichess Mobile
description: Official mobile app for Lichess. Play, solve puzzles, analyze games, and follow tournaments on the go.

name: BanksiaGUI
description: Feature-rich freeware chess GUI. Engine tournaments, database management, server connections, and game analysis.

name: PyChess Variants
description: Free online platform for chess variants. Play standard chess, Crazyhouse, Atomic, Shogi, and many more variants.

name: JFXChess
description: Cross-platform chess GUI with Stockfish engine. Game editing, PGN support, automatic analysis, and adjustable difficulty.

name: 365Chess
description: Online chess database with 4.4M+ games. Opening explorer, position search, puzzles, and endgame training tools.

name: Komodo Dragon
description: Top-tier chess engine combining neural network and traditional evaluation. World Computer Chess Champion with a human-like positional style.

name: HIARCS Chess Explorer
description: Chess database and analysis software with the multi-time World Champion HIARCS engine. Used by Vishy Anand and Garry Kasparov for preparation.

name: Tarrasch
description: Simple, portable desktop chess workbench. Edit PGN files, play against UCI engines, and analyze positions. Open-source C++ application.

name: DecodeChess
description: AI chess analysis that explains engine moves in plain language. Shows threats, plans, piece functions, and positional concepts using Stockfish NNUE.

name: Ethereal
description: Strong open-source UCI chess engine. Combines traditional evaluation with NNUE, serving as both competitive engine and reference for engine authors.

name: Tixati
description: Lightweight BitTorrent client with detailed bandwidth management. No ads, DHT, and advanced per-torrent settings.

name: aria2
description: Lightweight multi-protocol download utility. Supports HTTP, FTP, SFTP, BitTorrent, and Metalink with JSON-RPC control.

name: Private Internet Access
description: VPN service with open-source apps, MACE ad blocker, and configurable encryption. Large server fleet.

name: CyberGhost VPN
description: User-friendly VPN with optimized servers for streaming and torrenting. 100+ country locations.

name: Windscribe
description: VPN and ad-blocker combo with a generous free tier of 10GB/month. Browser extension and desktop clients.

name: Vuze
description: Feature-rich BitTorrent client with device playback, media search, and remote web access. Formerly Azureus.

name: PicoTorrent
description: Tiny, fast BitTorrent client for Windows. Native UI, IPv6 support, and very low resource usage.
